hero.horizonHint: That's the shortest time to reach your goals at the confidence you chose.

feasible.label: Achievable
feasible.description: Your plan reaches every goal on time.

tight.label: Tight
tight.description: Reachable, but with little margin. Consider raising your savings rate.

infeasible.label: Out of reach
infeasible.description: Your goals can't be met with the current plan. Adjust them and recalculate.

actions.hint: Update your situation or goals and recalculate to see a new plan.

job.queuedBody: We're getting ready to calculate. This usually starts in a few seconds.
job.runningBody: We're testing thousands of futures to find the shortest path to your goals.
job.failedBody: Your goals may be out of reach with the current savings rate. Try lowering a target, extending a date, or raising how much you save each month.

goals.title: Your goals
goals.subtitle: How likely each goal is to be reached, measured across thousands of simulated futures.
goals.allFloor_one: This goal is comfortably within reach — reached in nearly every scenario. Your certainty choice barely changes this plan; it mainly affects timing.
goals.allFloor_other: These goals are comfortably within reach — reached in nearly every scenario. Your certainty choice barely changes this plan; it mainly affects timing.
goals.chipTerminal: By the end of the plan
goals.onFloor: Reached in virtually every scenario — aiming for more certainty wouldn't change this goal.

contributions.title: Month-by-month contributions
contributions.subtitleWithdrawals: How much goes into each account each month, and when money is expected to come back out.
contributions.subtitle: How much to put into each account, every month.

wealth.title: Projected wealth
wealth.subtitleTotal: Likely range of your total savings over time. Darker band = more likely.
wealth.subtitleAccounts: Median line for each account with its likely range (P25–P75) shaded. Dashed lines show your goals.
wealth.subtitleAccount: Likely range for {{account}}. Darker band = more likely.

explainer.title: How this plan works
explainer.subtitle: The math is here if you want it, but each section translates the equation into plain language first.
explainer.hint: Hover or focus a symbol to highlight the matching part of the charts below. Click to pin it.
explainer.linkedConceptEmpty: Linked concept: hover a formula or chart element
explainer.spotlightWealth: {{label}} is active in the wealth equation and cash-flow visuals.
explainer.spotlightBands: {{label}} is active in the projected wealth distribution.
explainer.spotlightGoals: {{label}} is active in the goal logic for this plan.

wealth.title: Wealth evolution equation
wealth.intro: Each month, every account starts from its current balance, adds the planned contribution, subtracts any withdrawal, and then grows by that month's investment return.

var.wealth: wealth in account m at month t
var.contribution: money available to invest in month t
var.allocation: share of that month's contribution sent to account m
var.withdrawalActive: withdrawal taken from account m in month t
var.withdrawalZero: withdrawal term; zero in this scenario
var.return: return earned by account m during month t

wealth.calloutPre: In this plan, the optimizer is choosing the monthly split
wealth.calloutPost: across {{count}} accounts while respecting your goals, withdrawals, and time horizon.

bands.title: Confidence bands and uncertainty
bands.intro: The projected wealth chart is not one future. It summarizes many simulated futures and shows where the middle of the distribution sits and how wide the range becomes over time.

var.median: half of simulated outcomes are above it and half are below it
var.likely: the darker band; a tighter middle slice of outcomes
var.possible: the lighter band; a broader uncertainty envelope

bands.callout: Wider bands usually mean more uncertainty from returns, irregular investments, or withdrawals. Narrower bands mean outcomes are clustering more tightly.

goals.title: Goal probability and minimum horizon
goals.intro: FinOpt searches for the shortest horizon where your goals become achievable with the confidence levels you selected.

formula.horizon: smallest month count that satisfies all goals together

var.target: the threshold attached to a goal
var.horizon: the minimum feasible horizon; current result = {{horizon}} months

goals.callout: The goal list below shows each goal's likelihood, measured across the simulated futures. If one goal forces the plan to wait longer, it can determine the minimum horizon for the full plan.
